methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 10 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $200M - $3.5B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: QuantumScape
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Public), post-money $5B+

data.narrative:
LanzaTech is a publicly traded climate and energy company founded by Sean Simpson and Richard Forster. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 10 companies operating in the same sector and valuation tier, comprising 1 public comparable and 8 private-stage firms including Form Energy, Sila Nanotechnologies, KoBold Metals, Electric Hydrogen, and Amogy.

The peer-median method, the applicable triangulation approach given available data, establishes an implied valuation of $600M based on the median across the 10-company cohort. This peer universe spans a wide range—from $200M at the floor to $3.5B at the ceiling—reflecting the heterogeneity of capital intensity and commercialization readiness within frontier energy. Form Energy and Sila Nanotechnologies represent the high end at $3.5B and $3.3B respectively, while KoBold Metals, Electric Hydrogen, and Amogy occupy the middle band between $700M and $2.0B.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $510M to $690M, with a median of $600M. LanzaTech's stated valuation of $800M+ sits above this range, warranting scrutiny of company-specific factors not captured in peer comparables—including revenue traction, path to profitability, or differentiated technology position. Key uncertainties include the cyclicality of carbon markets, regulatory tailwinds, and execution risk on commercial deployment. The single-method reliance reflects data constraints; additional DCF or revenue-multiple approaches would strengthen conviction.
